title: Unsupervised Learning of Spatially-Resolved ARPES Spectra for Epitaxially Grown Graphene via Non-negative Matrix Factorization

paper_analysis.summary: This paper analyzes spatially resolved ARPES spectra from epitaxial graphene grown on SiC using non-negative matrix factorization (NMF) followed by k-means clustering. The method decomposes mixed spectra into basis components associated with monolayer, bilayer, and trilayer graphene features, and visualizes the spatial distribution of graphene thickness across the substrate. The main finding is that unsupervised learning can reproduce the measured spectra well and reveal spatial inhomogeneity without manual inspection.
measurement_types: k-means clustering on NMF activation vectors

figures.description: Basis vectors W obtained from NMF for the graphene ARPES dataset; these represent common spectral patterns and thickness-dependent Dirac-cone features.
Sum of squared errors between experimental and NMF-reconstructed spectra as a function of the number of basis vectors, used to choose five components by an elbow-like criterion.
Within-cluster sum of squared errors versus number of clusters for k-means on activation vectors, used to choose the cluster number.
Representative spectrum labeled as cluster 0, showing weak or absent graphene-derived features; includes experimental spectrum, NMF reconstruction, and activation-vector ratios.
Representative monolayer-graphene spectrum (cluster label 1) with experimental, reconstructed, and activation-vector panels.
Representative monolayer-graphene spectrum (cluster label 7) with different energy-direction intensity weighting than Fig3b.
Representative bilayer-graphene spectrum (cluster label 4) with experimental, reconstructed, and activation-vector panels.
Representative bilayer-graphene spectrum (cluster label 5) showing stronger contribution from one basis component than Fig3d.
Representative bilayer-graphene spectrum (cluster label 6) showing a different basis-component mixture from Fig3e.
Representative trilayer-graphene spectrum (cluster label 2) with a shifted Dirac-cone center and corresponding NMF reconstruction.
Representative trilayer-graphene spectrum (cluster label 3) with a larger angle shift than Fig3g.
Spatial map of cluster labels across the substrate, showing the distribution of graphene thickness/regions inferred from activation-vector clustering.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: The raw measurements are spatially resolved ARPES spectra collected on epitaxial graphene grown on n-doped 6H-SiC(0001). The dataset consists of approximately 400 spectra measured on an (x,z) grid with 0.5 mm and 0.2 mm step sizes, using a 40 eV incident photon energy at the Saga University beamline (BL13) of the SAGA Light Source. Each spectrum is indexed with its measurement location.
paper_analysis.processed_plot_details: The paper plots NMF basis spectra, reconstruction-error curves, k-means clustering error curves, representative experimental vs reconstructed spectra with activation-vector ratios, and a spatial label map. These are processed/derived from the raw ARPES spectra after binning, normalization, and background removal.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: The datasets generated during and/or analyzed during the current study are available from the corresponding author on reasonable request.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: Raw data are described as spatially resolved ARPES spectra measured on a graphene/SiC sample. The paper does not specify file formats, repository location, or whether the raw spectra are publicly deposited.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed data include binned spectra, normalized photoelectron intensities, background-removed spectra, flattened one-dimensional vectors stacked into a matrix for NMF, NMF basis vectors W, activation matrices H, reconstructed spectra, SSE curves for choosing the number of basis vectors, and k-means cluster labels/spatial maps.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Figure-specific data are clearly implied: Fig. 1 uses NMF basis vectors and SSE values; Fig. 2 uses clustering SSE values; Fig. 3 uses representative spectra, reconstructions, and activation-vector ratios; Fig. 4 uses spatial cluster labels. However, the paper does not state that separate source-data files are provided.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: Analysis was performed using NMF and k-means from the scikit-learn library in Python. The paper does not mention releasing scripts, notebooks, or code.
dataset_characterization.replot_reason: The paper does not provide a public dataset link, source-data archive, or explicit figure-data files. It only states that data are available from the corresponding author on reasonable request, so direct replotting from the paper alone is not possible.
dataset_characterization.notes: Important preprocessing steps were applied before NMF: spectra were binned to reduce size and noise, intensities were normalized, and background intensity was removed by setting intensities below the maximum background intensity to zero. The ARPES spectra were flattened into vectors for matrix factorization. The angle axis in the figures is treated as a dimensionless detected angle rather than converted momentum. The clustering labels are derived from NMF activation vectors, not from raw spectra directly.

classification_prior.raw_data_evidence: The Methods section states that the dataset included a spatial map of ARPES spectra measured on graphene/SiC, acquired at ~400 spatial points.
classification_prior.source_data_evidence: The Results section and figure captions show derived NMF basis vectors, SSE plots, reconstructed spectra, activation vectors, and spatial cluster maps, which are processed figure data.
classification_prior.both_evidence: Raw ARPES spectra are analyzed into processed NMF/clustering outputs used for figures.
classification_prior.data_availability_section_relevant: “The datasets generated during and/or analysed during the current study are available from the corresponding author on reasonable request.”; “The observed ARPES images were flattened into one-dimensional vectors and stacked to convert them into a two-dimensional matrix for applying NMF.”; “As a preprocessing step, a binning of spectra… The photoelectron intensities were normalized. The background intensity was removed…”
classification_prior.review_notes: Do not classify the NMF outputs as raw data. The paper strongly implies processed figure data but does not explicitly mention a downloadable source-data package or code release. The safest interpretation is that raw spectra exist, processed matrices/plots are used for figures, and public availability is limited to request from the corresponding author.
